name: Rosemary-Olive Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 4 Oil-packed dried tomatoes; drained and cut into strips
recipeInstructions: Preheat oven to 450°F. Place dough in bowl. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil and minced fresh rosemary. Season generously with pepper. Knead dough until ingredients are well combined. Roll dough out on lightly floured work surface to a 9x6-inch rectangle. Transfer to baking sheet. Flatten and press dough into a 12x9-inch rectangle. Rub 1 tablespoon olive oil over the dough. Sprinkle 3/4 cup Parmesan over; press gently into dough. Bake until bread is almost cooked through and cheese begins to brown, about 12 minutes. Arrange olives, tomatoes, and garlic atop the bread. Sprinkle remaining Parmesan to lightly cover. Drizzle remaining tablespoon olive oil over. Top with rosemary sprigs. Continue baking until cheese melts and bread is cooked through, about 5 minutes. Cut into squares or wedges and serve.
